title: Reading and Use of English - Part 6
description: You are going to read an article from which six sentences have been removed. Choose from the sentences A–G the one which fits each gap (37–42). There is one extra sentence which you do not need to use.

content.title: Growing Up: The Rise of Urban Farming
content.text: On a former industrial rooftop in Brooklyn, rows of leafy greens stretch under transparent panels while workers in wellington boots tend to beds of tomatoes, herbs and edible flowers. This is not a rural idyll; it is a working urban farm in the heart of one of the world's most densely populated cities. (37) [37] Iniciativas similares están surgiendo en ciudades de todo el mundo. [/37] From Singapore to Stockholm, entrepreneurs and city planners are reimagining what food production can look like in an urban environment. || The arguments in favour of urban farming are compelling. Conventional agriculture is responsible for a significant portion of global greenhouse gas emissions, and most supermarket produce travels thousands of kilometres before reaching a consumer's plate. (38) [38] Cultivar alimentos en la ciudad reduce drásticamente esa huella de carbono. [/38] A lettuce grown on a city rooftop and sold at a local market has a carbon footprint a fraction of the size of one shipped from southern Spain. || Technology has been central to urban farming's rapid development. Hydroponic and aeroponic systems allow crops to be grown without soil, using nutrient-rich water solutions instead. (39) [39] Estos métodos permiten producir más alimentos en menos espacio y con menos agua. [/39] Vertical farms, where crops are stacked in illuminated layers inside climate-controlled warehouses, can achieve yields up to forty times greater per square metre than traditional outdoor fields. || Not all urban farms rely on high technology, however. Community gardens — small, collectively managed plots on unused land — have flourished in low-income neighbourhoods where fresh produce is scarce. (40) [40] Estos huertos comunitarios también tienen un valor social y terapéutico innegable. [/40] Participants report improvements in mental wellbeing, stronger neighbourhood bonds and a renewed interest in cooking with fresh ingredients. || The commercial viability of urban farming remains a contested point. Energy costs for artificial lighting and climate control can be substantial, and start-up investment is high. (41) [41] Sin embargo, algunos proyectos han demostrado que el modelo puede ser rentable. [/41] Gotham Greens and Bowery Farming in the United States have attracted significant venture capital investment, suggesting that investors at least believe the numbers can work. || Whether urban farming will ever replace conventional agriculture is unlikely, but that is not necessarily the point. (42) [42] Su mayor valor puede estar en complementar el sistema alimentario actual, no en sustituirlo. [/42] Even a modest contribution to local food security, combined with the educational and environmental benefits, makes it a worthy addition to the modern city.

paragraphs.A: Growing food closer to where it is consumed dramatically reduces the emissions associated with transportation.
paragraphs.B: These methods can produce more food in less space while using a fraction of the water required by conventional farming.
paragraphs.C: Advances in LED lighting have made indoor crop production considerably cheaper over the past decade.
paragraphs.D: Its greatest value may lie in supplementing the existing food system rather than replacing it.
paragraphs.E: Community gardens offer participants social connection, mental health benefits and practical cooking skills.
paragraphs.F: Yet a number of commercial projects have already demonstrated that urban farming can turn a profit.
paragraphs.G: Similar projects are taking root in cities across Europe, Asia and the Americas.

questions.explanation: C generaliza el ejemplo de Brooklyn a otras ciudades del mundo, conectando con la frase siguiente sobre Singapur y Estocolmo.
A explica cómo cultivar en la ciudad reduce las emisiones de transporte, enlazando directamente con el ejemplo de la lechuga.
B describe los beneficios de los métodos hidropónicos y aeropónicos en espacio y agua, preparando los datos sobre las granjas verticales.
E añade la dimensión social y de bienestar de los huertos comunitarios, que el texto siguiente desarrolla con más detalle.
F introduce el contraste con 'sin embargo' ante las dudas sobre rentabilidad, y enlaza con los ejemplos de empresas con inversión.
D cierra el artículo con la idea de complementar, no sustituir, el sistema alimentario, reforzando la conclusión positiva.
